lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਭਰਿਪੁਰੇ ਰਹਿਆ ॥ ਜਲਿ ਥਲੇ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ॥ ਨਿਤ ਗਾਈਐ ਹਰਿ ਦੂਖ ਬਿਸਾਰਨੋ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: har bharipure rahiaa | jal thale raam naam | nit gaaeeai har dookh bisaarano |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Lord is totally permeating and pervading everywhere; The Name of the Lord is pervading the water and the land. So sing continuously of the Lord, the Dispeller of pain. ||1||Pause||
I have met the Guru, the Giver of liberation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੀਆ ਹੈ ਸਫਲ ਜਨਮੁ ਹਮਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: har keea hai safal janam hamaaraa |
lines.english: The Lord has made my life fruitful and rewarding.
I meditate on the Lord, the Dispeller of pain.
The Lord has made my life's journey fruitful and rewarding.
and your love of duality shall simply vanish.
Joining the Sangat, the Holy Congregation, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||1||
O mortal, place your hopes in the Name of the Lord,
And one who sings the Glorious Praises of the Lord's Name
One who, in hope, remains unattached to hope,
such a humble being meets with his Lord.
- servant Nanak falls at his feet. ||2||1||7||4||6||7||17||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Raag Bilaaval, Fifth Mehl, Chau-Padhay, First House:
How can I meet You, O Imperishable God?
He is attached to what he sees.
Have Mercy upon me, and place me upon the Path;
How can I cross over the poisonous world-ocean?
let me be attached to the hem of the robe of the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy. ||1||
The True Guru is the boat to carry us across. ||1||Pause||
They remain unaffected by pleasure and pain.
The wind of Maya blows and shakes us,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਭਗਤ ਸਦਾ ਥਿਰੁ ਸੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: har ke bhagat sadaa thir see |
lines.english: but the Lord's devotees remain ever-stable.
The Guru Himself is the Savior above their heads. ||2||
They burn to death in egotism, like the moth lured by seeing the flame.
Maya, the snake, holds all in her coils.
They make all sorts of decorations, but they do not find the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਹੋਇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੁ ਤਾ ਗੁਰੂ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaa hoe kripaal taa guroo milaavai |3|
lines.english: When the Guru becomes Merciful, He leads them to meet the Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਫਿਰਉ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਮੈ ਇਕੁ ਰਤਨੁ ਦਸਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: hau firau udaasee mai ik ratan dasaaeaa |
lines.english: I wander around, sad and depressed, seeking the jewel of the One Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਹਿ ਲਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: har kaa mandar tis meh laal |
lines.english: That jewel is within the body, the Temple of the Lord.
This priceless jewel is not obtained by any efforts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਿ ਖੋਲਿਆ ਪੜਦਾ ਦੇਖਿ ਭਈ ਨਿਹਾਲੁ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: gur kholiaa parradaa dekh bhee nihaal |4|
lines.english: The Guru has torn away the veil of illusion, and beholding the jewel, I am delighted. ||4||
he is like the mute, whose mind is filled with wonder.
One who has tasted it, comes to know its flavor;
I see the Lord, the source of bliss, everywhere.
Bilaaval, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਆਖਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ ॥੫॥੧॥
lines.translit: jan naanak har gun aakh samaaeaa |5|1|
lines.english: Servant Nanak speaks the Glorious Praises of the Lord, and merges in Him. ||5||1||
He has linked His servant to His service.
The Divine Guru has blessed me with total happiness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਘਨੁ ਨ ਲਾਗੈ ਜਪਿ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bighan na laagai jap alakh abhev |1|
lines.english: No obstacles block my path, meditating on the incomprehensible, inscrutable Lord. ||1||
He Himself is pervading everywhere;
The soil has been sanctified, singing the Glories of His Praises.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਤੁ ਗਇਆ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਏ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: durat geaa har naam dhiaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The sins are eradicated, meditating on the Name of the Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ਲਗੇ ਮਨਿ ਮੀਠੇ ॥
lines.translit: gur ke charan lage man meetthe |
lines.english: The Guru's Feet seem so sweet to my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ ਜਾ ਕਾ ਵਡ ਪਰਤਾਪੁ ॥
lines.translit: aad jugaad jaa kaa vadd parataap |
lines.english: from the very beginning, and throughout the ages, His Glory has been radiantly manifest.
By Guru's Grace, sorrow does not touch me. ||2||
The Supreme Lord God has become my Savior.
I found total peace, when the Guru was pleased. ||3||
He is unobstructed, dwelling everywhere.
Wherever I look, I see Him there with me.
O Nanak, the Lord and Master protects and cherishes His slaves. ||4||2||
You are the treasure of peace, O my Beloved God.
Bilaaval, Fifth Mehl:
